categories.oscillator: Modules that generate audio or modulation signals from oscillators or synthesis algorithms. Contains sound generators and modulators.
categories.sample_playback: Modules that play back audio samples
categories.container: Modules that hold and combine other sound generators
categories.sequencing: MIDI processors that generate or play back note sequences
categories.note_processing: MIDI processors that transform, filter, or react to incoming note events. Contains MIDI processors and modulators.
categories.dynamics: Effects that shape the amplitude or add distortion and saturation
categories.filter: Effects that shape the frequency spectrum of the audio signal
categories.delay: Effects based on delayed signal copies, including chorus and phaser
categories.reverb: Effects that simulate room acoustics and spatial reflections
categories.mixing: Effects that control volume, stereo width, or stereo balance. Contains effects and MIDI processors.
categories.input: Modulators that convert external events like MIDI or MPE into modulation signals
categories.generator: Modulators that create modulation signals internally, such as envelopes and LFOs. Contains modulators and MIDI processors.
categories.routing: Modules that forward, distribute, or proxy signals or events across the module tree. Contains sound generators, MIDI processors, effects, and modulators.
categories.utility: Modules for analysis, placeholders, or structural purposes without audio processing
categories.custom: Modules that run user-defined DSP logic via scriptnode networks, compiled C++ code, or HiseScript callbacks. Contains sound generators, MIDI processors, effects, and modulators.

Enables a lookup table for custom velocity response curves
Converts the output to a decibel scale (-100dB to 0dB) then to linear gain

modules.description: A waveform generator based on BLIP synthesis of common synthesiser waveforms.

parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.
The stereo balance
The maximum number of voices
The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer
The octave transpose factor for the first Oscillator
The waveform type for oscillator 1
The pitch detune of the first oscillator in cent
The stereo panning of the first oscillator
The octave transpose factor for the second Oscillator
The waveform type for oscillator 2
The pitch detune of the second oscillator in cent
The stereo panning of the second oscillator
The balance between the two oscillators
Can be used to mute the second oscillator to save CPU cycles
The pulse width of oscillator 1 for waveforms that support this (eg. square)
The pulse width of oscillator 2 for waveforms that support this (eg. square)
Syncs the second oscillator to the first
The semitone transpose amount for the first Oscillator
The semitone transpose amount for the second Oscillator

modulation.description: Modulates the output volume
Modulates the pitch of all voices
Modulates the balance between oscillator 1 and 2
Modulates the pitch of the second oscillator independently

modules.description: A two-dimensional wavetable synthesiser that morphs between waveforms using a table index and supports audio file resynthesis.

parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.
The stereo balance
The maximum number of voices
The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer
Enables higher-quality rendering with better interpolation at the cost of more CPU usage
Index of the currently loaded wavetable bank from the project's wavetable directory
Normalized position in the wavetable where 0.0 is the first and 1.0 is the last waveform
Updates the mipmap when pitch modulation goes outside the frequency range to reduce aliasing

modulation.description: Modulates the output volume
Modulates the pitch of all voices
Modulates the wavetable position as a scale factor on the table index value
Adds a bipolar offset to the wavetable position for modulation-driven morphing
